name: Search for a certificate to clone (certificate details)
pageTitle: Search for health or phytosanitary certificate - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
heading: Certificate details
bodyCopy: We will use these details to search for a matching digital health certificate.
secondaryActions: Choose date — icon button revealing the bespoke defra-datepicker dialog (accessible name from a sr-only span; never used by any test)
Previous month — button inside the date-picker dialog (date-picker__button__previous-month)
Next month — button inside the date-picker dialog (date-picker__button__next-month)
Cancel — closes the date-picker dialog (date-picker__button__close, value="close")
Dashboard — navigation link beside the Back link, outside main
layout: govuk-grid-row#search-health-certificate > govuk-grid-column-full; each field constrained individually with govuk-!-width-one-half. NOTE the id sits on the ROW, not the column (search.html:11).

form.note: Posts back to itself; on success proceeds to a results/summary page.

structure.text: We will use these details to search for a matching digital health certificate.
structure.govukComponent: Hint (rendered as div.govuk-hint, not a paragraph — used as page-level lede copy)
Date input (inside a Fieldset with legend govuk-fieldset__legend--m), augmented by the bespoke defra-datepicker

govukComponents.usage: Country of origin of certificate — 33 options (placeholder + 32 countries) WHEN cert-type is CHEDPP. Exposed to the tests as an ARIA combobox named 'Country of origin of certificate' (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:6-8), driven by value not label (selectOption({value}) at CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:55), so the option VALUES are ISO country codes. NOTE — the option list is cert-type dependent, not fixed: the CHED-P journey drives this same select with 'NZ' (see the country-of-origin field evidence). The 33 options below are the CHEDPP list only.
govukComponents.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — full option list re-read verbatim this pass; corroborated by /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/cloning/CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:6-8,55 (re-verified verbatim this pass)

govukComponents.usage: Certificate reference number, Consignor/consignee/importer name (both width-one-half), plus the Day/Month/Year date parts (width-2, width-2, width-4)
govukComponents.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — control inventory eval re-run this pass

govukComponents.usage: Certificate date of issue — 3 govuk-date-input__item wrappers holding Day/Month/Year text inputs with inputmode=numeric and maxlength 2/2/4. The wrapping div carries data-module="accessible-datepicker" (bespoke, not govuk-frontend). The tests address the three parts purely by accessible name 'Day' / 'Month' / 'Year' (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:14-24), i.e. the picker is never used.
govukComponents.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — 3 govuk-date-input__item; data-module=accessible-datepicker on DIV.govuk-date-input.date-input--picker (verified this pass); corroborated by CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:14-24,57-59

govukComponents.usage: Page lede copy, plus hints under country of origin and certificate reference number
govukComponents.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — count re-verified this pass (3)

govukComponents.usage: CORRECTED this pass — 7, not 4. Four TOP-LEVEL groups (country-of-origin, certificate-reference, the date fieldset wrapper, party-name) PLUS three NESTED groups, one inside each govuk-date-input__item wrapping Day, Month and Year. The nesting is standard govuk-frontend date-input markup (each date-input__item contains its own govuk-form-group), not an IPAFFS deviation — but the rebuild should expect 7 in the DOM.
govukComponents.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — eval over main .govuk-form-group returns 7; enumerated this pass: 4 at DOM depth 7 (country-of-origin, certificate-reference, date wrapper, party-name) and 3 at depth 11 (certificate-date-of-issue-day/-month/-year)

govukComponents.usage: Primary 'Search' submit — <button type="submit" value="Search" id="search" data-module="govuk-button" data-prevent-double-click="true"> with NO name attribute (confirmed). NOTE — a SECOND, variant-only submit labelled 'Continue' is exposed by the page object as btnContinue (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:34-36) and clicked by searchDigitalHealthCertificate() (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:44). That path also skips the country dropdown entirely, so a variant of this page renders WITHOUT country of origin and with 'Continue' rather than 'Search'. Its only caller is the amend-notification clone entry point (tests/notification/ched-a/ched-a-version.spec.ts:58, CHED-A, and the test is test.skip'd at :38), so no trace in our corpus rendered it. RE-VERIFIED this pass: a repo-wide grep for callers of searchDigitalHealthCertificate returns exactly one call site (ched-a-version.spec.ts:58) plus the definition, confirming the 'Continue' shape is unexercised by the entire suite. Note also cloning/search.html contains no Continue button at all, so this variant cannot be this template. Inferred, not confirmed; see openQuestions.
govukComponents.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — button outerHTML read verbatim this pass; search.html:67. Variant 'Continue' inferred from CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:34-36,38-45 + tests/notification/ched-a/ched-a-version.spec.ts:38,58-64

govukComponents.govukClass: n/a — rendered outside main, in the service-navigation bar
govukComponents.usage: 'Back' link to /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/cloning/type, followed by a '|' separator and a Dashboard link

NOT rendered on the clean page — not present in the DOM even hidden, so IPAFFS injects it server-side only on the error round-trip. Its slot is a `common/validationSummary` partial inside govuk-grid-column-full, directly above the H1. Included with count 0 so the rebuild knows the component belongs here and where it sits; its rendered copy is unverified (see validationMessages, all `inferred`).

Slot position from /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/cloning/search.html:13

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: defra-datepicker (with date-picker__container, date-picker__container--icon, date-picker__dialog, date-picker__dialog--hidden, date-picker__date-table, date-picker__header, date-picker__heading, date-picker__reveal__icon, date-picker__button__previous-month, date-picker__button__next-month, date-picker__button__close, date-input--picker, date-picker-day/-month/-year, date-input-day/-month/-year)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke DEFRA calendar-picker widget bolted on top of the standard GOV.UK date input. Renders a 'Choose date' reveal button (an svg icon plus a span.sr-only carrying the accessible name), which opens a div.date-picker__dialog[role=dialog][aria-modal=true][tabindex=-1]. The dialog holds a header with a 'Previous month' button, an h2.date-picker__heading[aria-live=assertive][aria-atomic=true] (observed: 'July 2026'), a 'Next month' button, then a table.date-picker__date-table[role=presentation] with a thead of weekday abbreviations (Su..Sa) and an EMPTY tbody, a 'Cancel' button (value="close") and a p.aria-live-message.sr-only[aria-live=assertive]. Selecting a date populates the three govuk-date-input text inputs, which carry extra hook classes (date-picker-day/-month/-year). CORRECTED this pass: the previous/next-month buttons have real DOM text — 'Previous month' and 'Next month' — they are NOT text-less '❮ ❯' glyph buttons as the previous pass stated. The '❮ ❯' the accessibility snapshot surfaces is CSS-generated presentation, and the underlying buttons are properly labelled.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a GOV.UK Design System component. The Design System deliberately has no date picker — its guidance is that three text inputs are more accessible and faster for known dates such as a certificate's date of issue, which the user reads off a document rather than browsing for in a calendar. This widget is bespoke JS/CSS the new app would have to own, test and maintain, and it is a11y risk surface (custom dialog, custom keyboard handling, custom aria-live). It also cannot work without JavaScript. A further a11y smell: the date grid is a <table role="presentation"> whose thead still uses <th scope="col"> — role=presentation strips the table semantics those th's are trying to provide, so the two are contradictory. Nothing in the observed journey requires the picker — the trace fills Day/Month/Year directly and never opens it. Corroborated by the QA automation: the page object has NO locator for the picker at all and every workflow fills the three text inputs directly (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:14-24,57-59), so the picker is unexercised by the whole regression suite as well as by the traces. RE-VERIFIED this pass by reading the page object in full — 63 lines, no picker locator, no dialog locator, no 'Choose date' reference.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Drop the picker entirely and keep the plain GOV.UK Date input (three text inputs with inputmode=numeric). If a picker is later shown to be a genuine user need, the DfE/MoJ 'Date picker' community component is the nearest thing to a supported option — but the default position for the rebuild should be no picker.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — full .date-picker__container outerHTML read verbatim this pass; /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/cloning/CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:1-63 (no picker locator exists anywhere in the file)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Sets the HTML autocomplete attribute on the two free-text inputs, passed through from the field name by the shared govuk/textInput partial.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Neither is a valid WHATWG autofill token, so browsers ignore both — the attribute is inert. It appears the field `name` was piped straight into `autocomplete` by the partial's caller rather than a real token being chosen. Harmless today but it is dead markup that implies an autofill behaviour that does not exist.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Omit autocomplete, or use a genuine token (e.g. autocomplete="organization" for the party name).
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — control inventory eval shows autocomplete=certificate-reference and autocomplete=party-name (re-verified this pass); /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/cloning/search.html:39,61

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Visually hides text: used both for the 'Choose date' reveal button's accessible name and for the picker's aria-live status paragraph.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bootstrap-era utility class, not part of govuk-frontend. Its presence means a non-GOV.UK CSS layer is loaded alongside govuk-frontend. Becomes moot if the date picker is dropped.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — span.sr-only inside button.date-picker__reveal__icon, and p.aria-live-message.sr-only (both read verbatim this pass)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The live-region paragraph the date picker writes status announcements into (p.aria-live-message.sr-only[aria-live=assertive], empty in the snapshot).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke class tied to the bespoke picker; disappears with it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: n/a — no equivalent needed once the picker is removed

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Float-clearing utility. CORRECTED this pass — it appears exactly once, on div.date-picker__header (the prev-button / month-heading / next-button row inside the picker dialog), NOT generally 'around the date input / picker block'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: It IS a real govuk-frontend class, but its use here signals a float-based layout inside the picker header rather than flex/grid. Minor; disappears with the picker.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard flex layout — no clearfix needed
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — the only .govuk-clearfix in main is DIV.date-picker__header.govuk-clearfix (verified this pass)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: div.govuk-hint used for the page lede ('We will use these details to search for a matching digital health certificate.')
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Page-level introductory copy is marked up as a hint rather than a body paragraph.
nonStandardPatterns.concern:
A govuk component used outside its intended role: govuk-hint is for field-level hint text associated with an input, not standalone page copy. It is not a

and is not associated with any control, so it reads as orphaned hint text. Trivial to correct in the rebuild.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — eval confirms tagName DIV, class govuk-hint (verified this pass); search.html:16

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The form occupies the full grid width, with per-field widths set via govuk-!-width-one-half.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not an error, but off-pattern: GOV.UK question pages are normally govuk-grid-column-two-thirds for line-length/readability. IPAFFS instead goes full-width and then halves each field. The rebuild should prefer two-thirds and drop the per-field width overrides.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — main .govuk-grid-row > div has className govuk-grid-column-full (verified this pass); search.html:12

fields.name: country-of-origin
fields.label: Country of origin of certificate
fields.hint: Select a country from the list of available countries
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — full option list re-read verbatim and re-counted this pass: 33 options in the exact order listed, placeholder 'Select a country' first, France carrying [selected]; action 15 selects it. Placeholder value is empty string (search.html:26). Required per ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/cloning/search_validation.js:34 (no HTML required attribute — validation is server-side only; re-verified: required=false on every control in the DOM). CORROBORATED by QA automation: the select is driven by VALUE (selectOption({value: country})) at CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:55, and the value passed is country.France = 'FR' (types/country.ts:80) via DEFAULT_CHEDPP_CLONING_CONFIG.country (ched-pp-workflows.ts:910 — re-verified this pass). Only 'FR' is ever exercised for CHED-PP — the other 31 options are unexercised by both the traces and the suite. CODE MAPPINGS (label→value) are NAME-MATCHED INFERENCES against types/country.ts, not observed pairings: the trace captured option LABELS only, and types/country.ts is the QA repo's own world-wide vocabulary, not this page's source. Verified codes in that vocabulary (all re-checked this pass): Canary Islands = 'ES-CN' (country.ts:43), Iceland = 'IS' (country.ts:105), Liechtenstein = 'LI' (country.ts:130), Norway = 'NO' (country.ts:169), Switzerland = 'CH' (country.ts:217). country.ts:110 is keyed `Ireland: 'IE'`, NOT 'Republic of Ireland' — the code is right but the QA repo does not use this page's label, so that pairing is inference by name, not confirmation. CERT-TYPE DEPENDENCE — MAJOR: this 33-option list is the CHEDPP list ONLY. The identical page object and select are driven by the CHED-P cloning workflow with country.NewZealand = 'NZ' (ched-p-workflows.ts:1288 + types/country.ts:160), and 'New Zealand' does NOT appear anywhere in the CHEDPP option list. That test is live (skipped only on the 'snd' environment, running on 'tst' — tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-cloning.spec.ts:11-14). So the options are injected server-side per cert-type, and the rebuild must NOT treat 32 EU/EEA countries as this select's fixed vocabulary. `inferred` for the cert-type dependence itself (no CHED-P render of this page was traced); `confirmed` for the CHEDPP list.

fields.name: certificate-reference
fields.label: Certificate reference number
fields.hint: You can get this from your export certifier
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 16 (fill) + action 21 (before) — no maxlength on this input. Required per search_validation.js:39-43. CORROBORATED: the value is the suite default DEFAULT_CHEDPP_CLONING_CONFIG.certificateReference = 'PHYTO.FR.2026.0000064' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:911), overridable per test via ChedPPCloningConfig.certificateReference (ched-pp-workflows.ts:899). NOTE the page object addresses the control by the accessible name 'Certificate reference' (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:10-12) — a substring match against the rendered label 'Certificate reference number', not a conflict. FORMAT IS CERT-TYPE DEPENDENT, three data points: CHED-PP 'PHYTO.FR.2026.0000064' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:911), CHED-P 'NZL2026/AGL18/3' (ched-p-workflows.ts:1289 — line CORRECTED this pass from 1287), CHED-A 'EXPORT.EU.FR.2025.0000068' (ched-a-version.spec.ts:59). Three mutually incompatible shapes on one shared input — so this field cannot carry a single format rule, and no format validation was observed on this page or found in search_validation.js (Joi.string().required() only, no .pattern()). For CHED-PP alone the observed shape is PHYTO.<ISO2>.<YYYY>.<7 digits>, but with n=1 that is a pattern, not a rule.

fields.name: certificate-date-of-issue-day
fields.label: Day
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 17 (fill) + action 21 (before) — maxlength=2, class 'govuk-input govuk-date-input__input govuk-input--width-2 date-picker-day' (re-verified verbatim this pass). Required via dateValidation({required:true}) in search_validation.js:58-67. CORROBORATED: addressed by accessible name 'Day' (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:14-16); value from DEFAULT_CHEDPP_CLONING_CONFIG.certificateDate = { day: '15', month: '04', year: '2026' } (ched-pp-workflows.ts:912), typed as DateInput = { day: string; month: string; year: string } (types/date-time.ts:1-5). Note the day is supplied UNPADDED ('15' is two digits anyway); the CHED-P default supplies '20' (ched-p-workflows.ts:1290 — line CORRECTED this pass from 1289), so no single-digit day is exercised anywhere and whether a bare '5' is accepted is untested.

fields.name: certificate-date-of-issue-month
fields.label: Month
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 18 (fill) + action 21 (before) — maxlength=2, class '... govuk-input--width-2 date-picker-month'. CORROBORATED: accessible name 'Month' (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:18-20); value '04' from ched-pp-workflows.ts:912 — note the suite supplies a ZERO-PADDED month, so zero-padding is at least accepted; whether a bare '4' is also accepted is untested. The CHED-P and CHED-A defaults also zero-pad ('04' at ched-p-workflows.ts:1290 — line CORRECTED this pass from 1289; '05' at ched-a-version.spec.ts:61), so every data point in the repo pads and the unpadded case has no evidence either way.

fields.name: certificate-date-of-issue-year
fields.label: Year
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 19 (fill) + action 21 (before) — maxlength=4, class '... govuk-input--width-4 date-picker-year'. CORROBORATED: accessible name 'Year' (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:22-24); value '2026' from ched-pp-workflows.ts:912.

fields.name: party-name
fields.label: Consignor, consignee or importer name
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 20 (fill) + action 21 (before) — no hint text rendered, no maxlength. Required per search_validation.js:48-51. CORROBORATED: accessible name matches the rendered label exactly (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:26-28); value from DEFAULT_CHEDPP_CLONING_CONFIG.partyName = 'WorldUSIN74 Co' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:913), overridable via ChedPPCloningConfig.partyName (ched-pp-workflows.ts:901). Other CHED types pass real trading names into the same input — 'ALLIANCE GROUP (NZ) LTD' (ched-p-workflows.ts:1291 — line CORRECTED this pass from 1290) and 'BIOLINE AGROSCIENCES LTD' (ched-a-version.spec.ts:63) — i.e. a free-text name matched against the certificate, with no observed lookup, autocomplete or case normalisation. Note the CHED-P value contains parentheses, so punctuation is at least accepted.

fields.name: cert-type
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — INPUT|hidden|cert-type. UPGRADED this pass: the previous pass said the value attribute 'was not read directly' and inferred CHEDPP from the URL. It has now been read directly from the frozen DOM — value="CHEDPP" — so the value is CONFIRMED, not inferred. Carries the CHED type chosen on the preceding cloning/type page and mirrors the ?certType=CHEDPP query param; rendered from the `certType` context variable (search.html:66). Optional in search_validation.js:52 (Joi.string().allow('').optional()). CORROBORATED by the workflow shape: the preceding page's radio choice determines the type — radioPlantsPlantProducts for CHED-PP (ched-pp-workflows.ts:939) vs radioProductsOfAnimalOrigin for CHED-P (ched-p-workflows.ts:1305), both landing on this same page object — so this page is shared across CHED types and cert-type is the only thing distinguishing them. RE-VERIFIED this pass: a repo-wide grep confirms exactly three callers of this page object (ched-pp-workflows.ts:942, ched-p-workflows.ts:1308, ched-a-version.spec.ts:58) and the cloning page objects themselves contain NO reference to certType/cert-type — the tests never read or assert it, so its effect is only visible through the differing country lists and reference formats each type supplies.

fields.name: crumb
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, action 21 (before) — INPUT|hidden|crumb, first child of the form (search.html:19), value is a 40-char token [REDACTED]. CSRF token (hapi crumb plugin). Platform concern, not a user-facing requirement; noted so the rebuild does not mistake it for data. NOTE a second `crumb` and the `returnUrl`/`fromFooterHeader` hidden inputs also exist in the document but belong to the cookie-banner form OUTSIDE main — they are not part of this page's data.

validationMessages.trigger: All four fields valid, but no digital health certificate matches the details given (wrong reference, expired certificate, or details that do not match the certificate on file)
validationMessages.evidence: NO evidence of the copy. That this state EXISTS is inferred from tests/notification/ched-a/ched-a-version.spec.ts:37-38,59 — the test is test.skip'd with the comment 'TODO enable this test, replace with a valid, non-expired certificate reference' and the inline '// Expired - needs valid reference', which tells us an expired or invalid reference does NOT reach the cloning summary page. Both re-read verbatim this pass. STRENGTHENED: the CHED-P cloning test is also skipped on the 'snd' environment with the reason 'No CHED-P certificate available on snd to clone from; runs on tst.' (tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-cloning.spec.ts:11-14, re-read verbatim this pass) — a second, independent acknowledgement that this page hard-fails when the certificate does not exist upstream, and that the suite's answer is to avoid the branch rather than assert on it. What IPAFFS renders instead (an error on this page? a 'no results' page? which field the error attaches to?) was never traced and is never asserted. This is the single biggest unknown on this page — it is the whole no-match branch of a search page. A human must answer it.

openQuestions: PROVENANCE OF THIS PASS — the page identity is re-confirmed, not assumed. Trace ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667 is 'notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts:10 › Cloned CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification' (48 actions, 1 error). The evidence pointer (action 17) lands on certificate-date-of-issue-day on this page and is correct. Action 21 is the 'before' phase of the Search click — the fully-populated form — and is the richest single snapshot; it is the primary citation throughout. The H1 in that snapshot is 'Certificate details', which matches this spec's `heading`: RIGHT PAGE.

CORRECTED this pass — Form group count was 4, actually 7. Four top-level groups plus three nested one-per-date-part inside the govuk-date-input__item wrappers. The nesting is standard govuk-frontend date-input markup, so this is a counting error in the spec, not an IPAFFS deviation.

UPGRADED this pass — the cert-type hidden input's value is now CONFIRMED as 'CHEDPP' by reading the value attribute directly out of the frozen DOM. The previous pass explicitly noted it 'was not read directly' and inferred it from the URL. No change to the conclusion, but the claim is now first-hand.

CORRECTED this pass — the previous pass described the picker's previous/next-month controls as 'rendered as ❮ ❯', which implied unlabelled glyph buttons. The DOM text is 'Previous month' and 'Next month'. The ❮ ❯ is CSS-generated presentation surfaced by the accessibility snapshot. This matters because it understated the picker's a11y quality on the one point where the picker is actually correct — the real a11y smells are elsewhere (see the next item).

NEW (DOM) — a11y smell inside the picker not previously recorded: the date grid is <table class="date-picker__date-table" role="presentation"> yet its thead still uses <th scope="col" abbr="Sunday" aria-label="Sunday">. role=presentation strips exactly the table semantics those th's supply, so the two contradict each other. The dialog itself is div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true][tabindex=-1] with an h2.date-picker__heading[aria-live=assertive][aria-atomic=true]. All bespoke, all unexercised. More weight behind dropping the picker.

NEW (source) — NO past-date rule applies to certificate date of issue. dateValidation only runs the today-or-in-past check when `checkIsInPast` is passed (date_validator.js:81-96), and search_validation.js:58-67 does NOT pass it — it passes only `required: true`. The codebase HAS the message (DATE_MUST_BE_TODAY_OR_IN_PAST_ERROR, validation_messages.js:24, applied at date_validator.js:187) and uses it elsewhere, so its absence here looks deliberate-or-forgotten rather than unavailable. CONSEQUENCE: a certificate 'issued' in the year 3000 is accepted by this page's validation and only fails at the upstream lookup. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: should the rebuild reject a future date of issue up front? A phytosanitary certificate cannot be issued in the future, so this looks like a real (if low-impact) validation gap. `legacy` — read from source (search_validation.js:58-67 passes only `required: true`, not `checkIsInPast`); never traced.

REFINED this pass — where date errors attach IS answerable from source, contrary to the previous pass's 'unverified from BOTH sources'. Range errors (Day/Month/Year out of range, year length, min year) attach to the PER-PART path `certificate-date-of-issue-{day,month,year}` via `${path}-${key}` at date_validator.js:151. Everything else (all-empty, incompleteness, not-a-real-date) attaches to the FIELDSET path `certificate-date-of-issue` (date_validator.js:102,123,177). The fields in validationMessages above reflect this. What remains unverified is only the RENDERED result — the error-summary title copy and how errorSpan/displayErrorClass present these — since no error state was ever traced.

NO error state for this page exists anywhere in this trace, so every validation message above is now tagged `legacy` (read verbatim from the authoritative frontend validation source — en.js, validation_messages.js, search_validation.js — NOT observed rendering). The previous pass tagged them `inferred` only because legacy source was not yet authorised; the copy is unchanged and re-verified this pass. Trustworthy for the COPY and the rule; the RENDERED presentation (error-summary title, per-field error span) remains unverified since no error state was ever traced. Every message's COPY was re-read verbatim from source this pass and all ten date constants plus the four en.js messages match their cited lines exactly. CORROBORATION FOUND NOTHING: a grep of the QA automation suite finds no assertion on any error message, error summary or field-error state for this page — the page object has no error locators at all. RE-CONFIRMED by reading CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts end-to-end (63 lines: 8 locators, 2 methods, zero error handling) and the sole CHED-PP cloning spec end-to-end. Worth confirming against an error-state trace if one exists in the other 37 error-bearing traces.

The date-of-issue error copy uses the literal label 'Date' (search_validation.js:63), so the messages read 'Date must include a day', NOT 'Certificate date of issue must include a day' — this looks like a copy defect in IPAFFS (the visible legend is 'Certificate date of issue'). Note the range errors hardcode 'Day'/'Month'/'Year' independently of that label (date_validator.js:136,139,164,168), so they are unaffected. The rebuild should use the real field name. Flagging rather than silently correcting.

RESOLVED — the V2 question below is ANSWERED by the page template at /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/cloning/search.html (re-read in full this pass). It is a FEATURE FLAG, `isEnableCloningUpdates`, gating exactly three things: the lede copy (search.html:15-17), the whole date-of-issue field (search.html:43-52) and the party-name field (search.html:54-63). Flag OFF => country + certificate reference only, which is precisely the 2-field shape of `validate`; flag ON => all four fields, matching `validateV2`. So `validate`/`validateV2` ARE old-vs-new versions of THIS one page, selected by that flag — and the trace observed the flag ON. IMPLICATION FOR THE REBUILD: build the four-field (flag-on) shape; the two-field shape is legacy behind a flag that should not be carried over. Confirm with a human that the flag is now permanently on in production. SUPPORTING EVIDENCE FROM THE QA SIDE: every live workflow fills all four fields (ched-pp-workflows.ts:942-949, ched-p-workflows.ts:1308-1315), so the flag is on in every environment the suite runs against — consistent with the flag being effectively permanent.

SEPARATE, AND STILL OPEN — the 'Continue'/no-country variant described below is NOT the flag-off shape (flag-off still has country, and still says 'Search'). It has no country dropdown AND a Continue button, neither of which this template can ever render — RE-CONFIRMED this pass by reading search.html end-to-end: its only button is <button id="search" value="Search">Search</button> at :67. It is therefore a DIFFERENT TEMPLATE, most likely src/views/importer/retrospectiveCloningSearch.html or src/views/importer/latestHealthCertificate.html. That page is out of scope for this slug, but the parent should check it is covered by some slug, since the page object conflates the two. Original note follows:

There are TWO validators in search_validation.js: `validate` (country + certificate reference only, :13-30) and `validateV2` (adds date of issue + party name, :32-70). The rendered page has all four fields, so V2 is live. The QA page object carries BOTH shapes as separate methods — selectCountryAndReferenceNumberAndSearch() (country + reference + date + party + 'Search', CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:47-62) and searchDigitalHealthCertificate() (reference + date + party + 'Continue', NO country, CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:38-45). The 4-field/'Search' shape is reached from the dashboard 'Clone' flow via cloning/type (ched-pp-workflows.ts:937-949 and ched-p-workflows.ts:1303-1315); the 3-field/'Continue' shape is reached from an EXISTING notification's amend screen ('Clone digital health certificate', ched-a-version.spec.ts:57-64) — where the CHED type is already known from the notification, which would explain the missing country/type step. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: are these genuinely two variants of the same page, and does the amend entry point apply to CHED-PP too, or only CHED-A? Its only caller is a CHED-A test and that test is test.skip'd (ched-a-version.spec.ts:38), so it is unexercised by BOTH the traces and the live suite — I cannot confirm it renders at all, let alone for CHED-PP. RE-VERIFIED this pass: a repo-wide grep for searchDigitalHealthCertificate returns exactly one call site plus the definition, so nothing has changed — this remains a single skipped CHED-A test as the only evidence for a whole page variant.

The country list is CERT-TYPE DEPENDENT. The 32 countries are the CHEDPP list. The CHED-P cloning workflow drives the SAME page object and the SAME select with country.NewZealand = 'NZ' (ched-p-workflows.ts:1288, types/country.ts:160) — and New Zealand is absent from the CHEDPP list. That test is live (it skips only on 'snd', and runs on 'tst' — ched-p-cloning.spec.ts:11-14), so this is not dead code. CONSEQUENCE: the `countries` context variable this select renders from is populated per cert-type by the handler, and CHEDPP's list is a phytosanitary-specific subset (EU/EEA) while CHED-P's is a third-country list. QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: what determines the CHEDPP subset — is it 'countries whose NPPO issues digital phytos IPAFFS can retrieve from TRACES/EUROPHYT', or a static config? The rebuild must source this from a reference-data service keyed by CHED type, NOT hardcode 32 countries. This also explains why no UK/GB entry appears (see the ukRegions note below): a UK-issued phyto is not a thing you clone on an import.

The country list is 32 countries — EU member states plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, and Canary Islands (ES-CN) as a separate entry from Spain (ES). It is notably NOT a full world list, i.e. it is scoped to countries that issue digital phytosanitary certificates IPAFFS can retrieve. PARTIALLY RESOLVED — it is NOT hardcoded in the template: search.html:25-28 renders a `data/countriesSelectHighLow` partial (countries=countries regions=ukRegions country=form.country-of-origin) below a hardcoded <option value="">Select a country</option>, i.e. only the placeholder is in the template and the list is injected by the handler from an upstream source. So the subsetting to 32 happens server-side, above the view. What feeds `countries` for this route (a reference-data service, a static config, or a TRACES lookup) was not traced and is still open. Either way the new app should source it from a reference-data service. CORROBORATION: the QA repo's own country vocabulary (types/country.ts) is a much larger, world-wide list (it includes NorthernIreland 'GB-NIR' at country.ts:239, England, etc.), so it is NOT the source of this page's 32 — it confirms the page's list is a deliberate subset chosen by the app, not by the tests. Only 'FR' is ever exercised.

All label→code pairings in the options list are name-matched INFERENCES, not confirmed pairings — the trace captured option LABELS only. Note country.ts:110 is keyed `Ireland: 'IE'`; the QA repo nowhere uses this page's label 'Republic of Ireland'. The only label→value pairing actually confirmed end-to-end is France = 'FR' (selected by value in the trace, and shown [selected] in the snapshot). If the rebuild needs the real value list, read it from the reference-data source, not from this spec's option labels.
No govuk-error-summary is present in the DOM at all on the clean page (not even hidden), so IPAFFS renders it server-side only on the error round-trip. Re-verified this pass.

Neither `required` nor `aria-required` is set on any control — all four fields are enforced server-side only. Re-verified this pass via the control-inventory eval (required=false on every input and the select). This is consistent with GOV.UK practice, but recorded so the rebuild does not read `required=false` from the DOM as 'optional'. MANDATORINESS IS `legacy`, NOT `confirmed`: each field's EXISTENCE, label and value are confirmed-rendered, but its REQUIREDNESS is sourced only from the frontend Joi validator (validateV2, search_validation.js:34,39,48,58-67 — all four of country-of-origin, certificate-reference, certificate-date-of-issue and party-name are `.required()` / `required:true`; cert-type is `.allow('').optional()` at :52). That all four search inputs are mandatory is a POLICY the rebuild may revisit — it is how tightly the old system narrowed the certificate match, not a fixed requirement; a rebuild could, for example, make party-name optional if reference+country+date already identify the certificate. Flagging as a legacy policy, not silently carrying it over.

CORRECTED this pass — the previous pass said the Search button had 'no name/value observed'. It has NO name attribute but it DOES carry value="Search": <button class="govuk-button govuk-!-margin-top-7" type="submit" value="Search" id="search" data-module="govuk-button" data-prevent-double-click="true">Search</button>. The form posts back to itself (action="search" method="POST") and on success proceeds to a results page. The trace's only error is at action 22 ('Clone' button, 30s timeout) which is on the results page downstream of this one, not here.

The maxlength on day/month/year (2/2/4) already prevents most range errors client-side, which is why the range messages are hard to trigger and likely why no trace ever rendered them. Note maxlength is not a substitute for validation — it caps length, not range, so 'Day must be from 1 to 31' is still reachable with e.g. 99.

Accessibility coverage gap: tests/accessibility/ contains NO reference to any cloning page (a case-insensitive grep for 'clon' across that directory returns zero hits — RE-CONFIRMED this pass). So this page — and the whole clone journey — is outside the a11y regression suite, despite carrying the most bespoke, highest-a11y-risk widget in the journey (the defra-datepicker). The rebuild should bring this page into a11y coverage from day one.

The whole page is exercised by exactly ONE happy path per CHED type, with one hardcoded certificate ('PHYTO.FR.2026.0000064', ched-pp-workflows.ts:911, dated 15/04/2026). Every branch other than 'four valid fields, certificate found' is untested and untraced: no-match, expired certificate, wrong party name, malformed reference, any of the other 31 countries. Treat this page's requirements as a thin happy path plus a large acknowledged unknown, not a complete picture. RE-CONFIRMED this pass by reading the sole CHED-PP cloning spec (tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts:1-28) end-to-end: it asserts NOTHING about this page. Its only assertions are downstream — that the resulting reference matches the CHED-PP pattern (:16) and that a Dynamics record exists for it (:24-25). So this page has ZERO direct test assertions from any source; it is only ever traversed on the way to something else.

The clone journey depends on certificate test data that must pre-exist in TRACES, and the suite openly cannot keep it valid: the CHED-A clone test is permanently skipped on an expired reference (ched-a-version.spec.ts:37-38,59) and the CHED-P clone test is skipped on whole environments for want of a certificate (ched-p-cloning.spec.ts:11-14). Only CHED-PP currently has a live, unconditional clone test. This is a testability finding for the rebuild: whatever replaces this page needs a stub/seam for the upstream certificate lookup, or its clone journey will rot the same way. A human should confirm the new app plans one.

(template) — the countriesSelectHighLow partial is passed `regions=ukRegions` as well as `countries=countries` (search.html:27), yet no UK/GB entry renders for CHED-PP. So either ukRegions is empty for this route or the partial's 'high' group is unused here. Unresolved; noted because a 'high/low' grouped select usually implies pinned-to-top entries, and none appeared. See the cert-type-dependence note above for a likely explanation.

(template) — the error summary's position is pinned: `common/validationSummary` renders at search.html:13, i.e. INSIDE govuk-grid-column-full and directly ABOVE the H1. Per-field error hooks also exist: `displayErrorClass errors 'country-of-origin'` on the form-group (search.html:21) and `errorSpan errors 'country-of-origin'` between hint and select (search.html:24); the govuk/textInput and govuk/date partials each receive `errors=errors`. Still no evidence of any rendered error copy or the summary heading (see the validation note above), but the rebuild knows where it all goes.

(DOM) — the page <title> ('Search for health or phytosanitary certificate - Import and export applications - GOV.UK') does NOT match the H1 ('Certificate details'). This breaks the GOV.UK convention that the title should lead with the H1, and it means the browser tab / screen-reader page announcement describes a different page from the one on screen. Confirmed from the snapshot this pass, not a guess. Fix in the rebuild — and note the <title> is arguably the BETTER copy of the two, since 'Certificate details' is ambiguous against the many other certificate pages in this journey.

(DOM) — the H1 is literally 'Certificate details ' with a TRAILING SPACE, in the template (search.html:14) and in the rendered DOM (h1.textContent === 'Certificate details ' — re-verified byte-for-byte this pass). Transcribed without it in `heading`; recorded here so nobody treats a trailing-space mismatch in a test assertion as a bug in their own code.

(DOM) — the date-picker's day grid renders with a completely EMPTY <tbody> in the snapshot; the cells are injected by client-side JS only when the dialog is revealed. Re-verified this pass (0 tbody rows). Combined with the fact that no trace or test ever opens the picker, the picker's calendar has NO evidence behind it at all — not its cell markup, not its keyboard behaviour, not its a11y contract. Further weight behind dropping it and keeping the plain Date input, which works with no JS.

(DOM) — the date input wrapper carries data-module="accessible-datepicker" (a bespoke IPAFFS module name, NOT a govuk-frontend one) and the Search button data-module="govuk-button" plus data-prevent-double-click="true" (both re-verified this pass; the button had also been initialised, data-govuk-button-init=""). The double-click guard is worth keeping in the rebuild: this page triggers a slow upstream call to TRACES, so double submission is a real risk (the trace's 30s timeout downstream shows how slow that path can be).

The ChedPPCloningConfig interface (ched-pp-workflows.ts:897-907) marks every one of this page's inputs (country, certificateReference, certificateDate, partyName) as OPTIONAL with a default — that is test ergonomics, NOT a statement that the fields are optional in the UI. Recorded so nobody reads the '?' as evidence against `required: true`. No conflict with the trace. RE-VERIFIED this pass: the interface fields are genuinely all `?` and DEFAULT_CHEDPP_CLONING_CONFIG is typed `Required<ChedPPCloningConfig>` (ched-pp-workflows.ts:909), i.e. the optionality exists purely so a test can override one field — every field is always supplied at call time (ched-pp-workflows.ts:942-949).

The VALUES were all verbatim-correct — only the pointers drifted. Every other citation in this spec was re-checked line-by-line this pass and holds exactly: en.js:47,48,49,63; validation_messages.js:14-23; date_validator.js:102,112-117,136,139,151,164,168,177; validation_constants.js:327-331; search_validation.js:34,39,48-52,58-67; search.html:11-19,21,24-28,39,43-52,54-63,66-67; CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts:6-8,10-12,14-24,26-28,30-36,38-45,47-62; ched-pp-workflows.ts:897-913,939,942-949; ched-p-workflows.ts:1288,1305,1308; ched-a-version.spec.ts:37-38,56,58-64; country.ts:43,80,105,110,130,160,169,217,239; ched-p-cloning.spec.ts:11-14; ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts:16,24-25. Note the page object is 63 lines, not 62 as previously stated.

No discrepancy between the traces and the tests was found on this page. The substantive corrections this pass are the form-group count (4→7), the cert-type value upgrade (inferred→confirmed), the picker prev/next button description, the Search button's value attribute, the govuk-clearfix host, and the grid id placement; the substantive additions are the missing past-date rule, the role=presentation/th contradiction, and the resolved error-attachment paths. All copy — H1, title, lede, both hints, all six labels, the legend, all 33 option labels in order, and all fifteen validation messages — was re-read verbatim against the DOM or source this pass and every string in the previous spec matched exactly. No invented copy was found.
